title: Detective Work
subtitle: Investigate a week of server logs

sections.instruction:
You're an ops engineer investigating a bad week. The server had problems, and your job is to figure out what happened using the terminal tools you've learned.

You have 5 days of logs in the `logs/` folder and source code in `src/`. Use `grep`, `wc`, pipes, and everything else to investigate.
sections.prompt: Find all the errors across the week. Which day had the most? Use `grep -c` to count errors per file, or `grep -r` to search all logs. Also check the source code for TODOs.
sections.hint: Try: grep -c "ERROR" logs/monday.log or grep -r "ERROR" logs/
hints: Use grep -r "ERROR" logs/ to search all log files at once
Use grep -c "ERROR" logs/*.log to count errors per file
The answer: wednesday.log had the most errors (5)
To find TODOs in source: grep -r "TODO" src/

onSuccess.message:
Excellent detective work! You used grep, pipes, and wc to analyze real log data across multiple files. Wednesday was the bad day — 5 errors including disk full, memory issues, and a Redis outage. And you found those TODOs in the source code that still need fixing.

These are the exact skills ops engineers and developers use every day to diagnose production issues.

completionMessage: Level 2 complete! You can now read files, write to them, search across entire projects, and chain commands into powerful pipelines.

milestone.title: Reading and Writing Files
summary: Read file contents with cat, head, and tail
Write and append to files with > and >>
milestone.nextLevelTeaser: Next: Level 3 — Your Code Has a History. Learn git and never lose your work again.
